# Reproduce, Revise, and Reuse: practical examples

Author

Affiliation

[Eduard Bukin](https://github.com/ebukin) [](mailto:ebukin@worldbank.org) [![](data:image/png;base64,)](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1003-1260)

Distributional Impact of Policies. Fiscal Policy and Growth Department

This example demonstrates how to use AI to understand and review data. it uses [Self-study Example 3](../selfstudy/example-3.llms.md) as a starting point, and expands on it by introducing **Plan mode** and features such as **MCP**, **Tools**, and **Prompt files** as well as **skills**.

## Overview

This workflow incorporates best practices from:

- Claude’s [Common workflows](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/common-workflows) and [Best practices for code review](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/best-practices), which include:
- Day 1’s [Common workflows](../methods/common-workflow.llms.md).

## Step 1. Reproduce: Understand the code and data transformation to reproduce the analysis

### Research the code

``` numberSource
Audit this analysis code end‑to‑end. Produce a structured walkthrough
with sections: Inputs, Outputs, Step‑by‑step pipeline, Key
assumptions/decision points, Validation checks, Potential issues.
In the pipeline section, describe the data flow as “A → B → C”,
naming intermediate datasets/objects and the operations applied.
```

### Reproduce the code

Ok prompt that contains ambiguity example:

``` numberSource
Ran this code and produce the output. If it fails, identify the error and
fix it iteratively until it runs successfully. Then, produce the output.
```

Better prompt example:

- Explicitly mention the tool to use
- If any other tool is needed, ask to explain why it is proposed and how it will work before request is submitted for my approval.

``` numberSource
Execute script attached using #executeCode tool. If it fails, identify the error
and fix it iteratively until it runs successfully. Then, produce the output.
If you need to use any other tool, explain why it is needed and how it will
work before requesting my approval.
```

## Step 2. Plan: review

``` numberSource
/plan revising `analysis.do` to incorporate new wave of the survey data, 2024.
Correct appropriate parts of the code and execute it iteratively until it runs
successfully. Then, produce the output and summarize for me the changes you made
and how you revised the code to accommodate the new data. Also summarize
how outputs look like now. If you need to use any tools, explain why they are
needed and how they will work before requesting my approval.
```
